sanskrit:
स्वभावजेन कौन्तेय निबद्धः स्वेन कर्मणा।
कर्तुं नेच्छसि यन्मोहात्करिष्यस्यवशोऽपि तत्॥
transliteration:
svabhāva-jena kaunteya nibaddhaḥ svena karmaṇā
kartuṃ necchasi yan mohāt kariṣyasy avaśo ’pi tat
literal_meaning: svabhāva-jena—by one’s own nature; kaunteya—O son of Kuntī; nibaddhaḥ—bound; svena—by one’s own; karmaṇā—action; kartum—to do; na—not; icchasi—you wish; yat—that; mohāt—by illusion; kariṣyasi—you will do; avaśaḥ—helplessly; api—even; tat—that.
contextual_meaning: Even when the ego believes it is choosing, the deeper samskāras (imprints) will drive behavior; resistance is only a surface ripple over an already-moving current.
psychological_state: Pre-contemplation stage of change: conscious refusal masking unconscious compliance with entrenched patterns.
trigger_scenario: Promising yourself to disconnect from work email after 7 p.m., yet automatically reaching for the phone at 9:30 p.m. while insisting you 'won't answer'.
anchor_text: Your nature will act through you even when you think you resist.
